evals.prompt: I want to contribute a new theme to openstatus. I'm thinking something Solarized-inspired — warm beige background, muted terminal feel. Help me build it and open a PR.
evals.expected_output: Should clarify intent (confirm contribution path), design the palette in stages (base, brand, status, charts), propose OKLCH values with rationale, write packages/theme-store/src/solarized.ts, update index.ts, and outline PR steps (branch name, title, description).
assertions: Clarifies this is a contribution, not a personal customization
Proposes palette in stages rather than dumping all 50 values
Uses OKLCH color space with explanation
Creates file at packages/theme-store/src/solarized.ts (or similar kebab-case)
Theme object uses `as const satisfies Theme`
Imports Theme type from ./types
Registers the new theme in packages/theme-store/src/index.ts THEMES_LIST
Provides PR title, branch name, and description template

evals.prompt:
I configured a theme on themes.openstatus.dev and want to wire it into the repo. Here's the export:

```
id: sunset
name: Sunset
author: @alice, https://alice.dev
light:
  --background: oklch(98% 0.02 80)
  --foreground: oklch(22% 0.04 30)
  --primary: oklch(55% 0.2 30)
  --success: oklch(68% 0.18 150)
  --warning: oklch(78% 0.17 70)
  --destructive: oklch(58% 0.24 27)
  --info: oklch(60% 0.19 260)
dark:
  --background: oklch(16% 0.03 30)
  --foreground: oklch(95% 0.02 80)
  --primary: oklch(70% 0.2 30)
  --success: oklch(72% 0.19 150)
  --warning: oklch(78% 0.17 70)
  --destructive: oklch(70% 0.19 22)
  --info: oklch(62% 0.19 260)
```
evals.expected_output: Should convert the pasted config into a valid TypeScript file at packages/theme-store/src/sunset.ts, preserve the values exactly, structure with `as const satisfies Theme`, and register in index.ts. Should also mention the next steps for testing and PR.
assertions: Creates file at packages/theme-store/src/sunset.ts
Preserves all pasted color values exactly (no silent edits)
Sets id: "sunset", name: "Sunset", correct author object
Uses `as const satisfies Theme` with Theme imported from ./types
Updates THEMES_LIST in index.ts with an import + append
Mentions local testing steps (pnpm dev:status-page, sessionStorage tip)

evals.prompt: I'm self-hosting openstatus and just want to change the colors to match my brand (primary #ff5722, dark blue #0a2540 backgrounds). Not planning to submit a PR. What's the simplest path?
evals.expected_output: Should recognize this is personal-not-contribution, offer the scaffold as a local theme in packages/theme-store/src/ but skip PR guidance, explain how to select it locally. Should NOT push contribution workflow.
assertions: Recognizes 'personal' path and skips PR-specific steps
Still scaffolds a theme file with proper Theme shape
Generates both light and dark mode values (not just one)
Explains how to preview locally (dev:status-page + sessionStorage)
Does not assume or suggest opening a pull request

evals.expected_output: Should flag specific problems: foreground vs background contrast in light mode is too low (L 90 vs L 65 ≈ 2.5:1, fails AA), status colors are all in a narrow lightness range so they'd look similar under colorblind simulation, dark mode is missing status colors entirely. Propose concrete fixes.
assertions: Identifies low contrast between light-mode background and foreground
Notes that status colors are too close in lightness/chroma to be distinguishable
Flags that dark mode is missing --success/--warning/--destructive/--info
Proposes concrete OKLCH adjustments (specific numbers, not vague advice)
Does NOT just rubber-stamp the theme as accessible

evals.prompt: I want to change the font on my status page to Inter
evals.expected_output: Should recognize this is not a theme (theme-store only handles color CSS variables, not typography) and redirect the user to the right place (apps/status-page/src/app/globals.css or the Next.js font setup).
assertions: Does not scaffold a theme file
Explains that theme-store controls colors/radius, not typography
Points user to where font configuration lives (apps/status-page)

evals.prompt: My company's brand colors are #0066ff (primary blue) and #00cc88 (secondary green). Generate a complete openstatus theme from those.
evals.expected_output: Should take the two brand colors and derive a full coherent palette: pick neutral backgrounds (white/off-white light, near-black dark), use #0066ff as --primary, possibly reuse the green as --success, pick sensible --warning and --destructive that don't clash with the brand blue, propose both modes. Write the file and register it.
assertions: Uses #0066ff as --primary (not silently replaced with a different color)
Uses the green #00cc88 meaningfully (either --success or --secondary, with rationale)
Picks neutral low-chroma backgrounds rather than tinting heavily
Provides all four status colors (--success, --warning, --destructive, --info)
Covers both light and dark mode
Writes a registered theme file
